Description: Use the CLI to initialize shadcn/ui with correct configuration
Do: Run npx shadcn@latest init
Code Good:
npx shadcn@latest init
# Follow prompts for:
# - TypeScript
# - Style (default/new-york)
# - Base color
# - CSS variables
Code Bad:
# Manually copying files
cp -r shadcn-ui/components ./components
# Missing configuration

Description: components.json defines paths and styling options
Do: Set correct aliases and paths
Don't: Use default paths that don't match project

Description: Add components individually using the CLI
Do: Install components you need with CLI
Code Good:
npx shadcn@latest add button
npx shadcn@latest add card
npx shadcn@latest add dialog
Code Bad:
npx shadcn@latest add --all
# Bloats project with unused components

Description: The cn utility combines class names properly
Do: Use cn() for all conditional classes
Code Good:
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'
<div className={cn(
  'base-class',
  isActive && 'active-class',
  className
)}>
Code Bad:
<div className={`base-class ${isActive ? 'active-class' : ''} ${className}`}>
// Messy, no deduplication

Description: shadcn/ui uses CSS custom properties for theming
Do: Define colors as CSS variables in globals.css
Code Bad:
<Button className="bg-[#1a1a2e] text-white">
  <!-- Hardcoded, won't theme -->
</Button>

Description: Implement dark mode with class or system preference
Do: Use next-themes or similar for mode switching

Description: Apply colors by their purpose not their appearance
Do: Use primary secondary destructive etc
Code Good:
<Button variant="destructive">Delete</Button>
<Badge variant="secondary">Draft</Badge>
<Alert variant="default">Info</Alert>
Code Bad:
<Button className="bg-red-500">Delete</Button>
<Badge className="bg-gray-200">Draft</Badge>
// Won't follow theme

Description: Components are copied to your project for customization
Do: Import from @/components/ui
Code Good:
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { Card } from '@/components/ui/card'
Code Bad:
import { Button } from 'shadcn/ui'
// Package doesn't exist

Description: Components are built with composition patterns
Do: Compose components with children and slots
Don't: Expect single component with many props
Code Good:
<Card>
  <CardHeader>
    <CardTitle>Title</CardTitle>
    <CardDescription>Description</CardDescription>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>Content</CardContent>
  <CardFooter>Footer</CardFooter>
</Card>
Code Bad:
<Card
  title="Title"
  description="Description"
  content="Content"
  footer="Footer"
/>
// Not how shadcn works

Description: Components should forward refs for DOM access
Do: Use forwardRef and spread props
Code Good:
const Button = React.forwardRef<
  HTMLButtonElement,
  ButtonProps
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <button ref={ref} {...props} />
))
Button.displayName = 'Button'

Description: Use open and onOpenChange for controlled dialogs
Do: Use controlled state for programmatic control
Don't: Rely only on trigger for opening
Code Good:
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
<Dialog open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <DialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button>Open</Button>
  </DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>...</DialogContent>
</Dialog>
Code Bad:
<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger>Open</DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>...</DialogContent>
</Dialog>
// Can't open programmatically

Description: Include required accessibility elements in dialogs
Do: Add DialogHeader DialogTitle DialogDescription
Code Good:
<DialogContent>
  <DialogHeader>
    <DialogTitle>Edit Profile</DialogTitle>
    <DialogDescription>
      Make changes to your profile here.
    </DialogDescription>
  </DialogHeader>
  {/* content */}
</DialogContent>
Code Bad:
Edit Profile

Make changes here

 // Missing aria labels

Description: Properly close dialog after form submission
Do: Close dialog in onSubmit handler
Code Good:
<form onSubmit={(e) => {
  e.preventDefault()
  handleSubmit()
  setOpen(false)
}}>
  <DialogFooter>
    <Button type="submit">Save</Button>
  </DialogFooter>
</form>
Code Bad:
<form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
  <Button type="submit">Save</Button>
</form>
// Dialog stays open

Description: Sheet provides slide-out panels from edges
Do: Use Sheet for navigation settings filters
Don't: Use Dialog for side panel content
Code Good:
<Sheet>
  <SheetTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Filters</Button>
  </SheetTrigger>
  <SheetContent side="right">
    <SheetHeader>
      <SheetTitle>Filters</SheetTitle>
    </SheetHeader>
    <FilterForm />
  </SheetContent>
</Sheet>
Code Bad:
<Dialog>
  <DialogContent className="fixed right-0">
    <!-- Trying to make dialog a sheet -->
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

Description: Sheet can slide from left right top or bottom
Do: Set side prop based on UX needs
Code Good:
<SheetContent side="left">
  <!-- Navigation panel -->
</SheetContent>
<SheetContent side="bottom">
  <!-- Mobile action sheet -->
</SheetContent>
Code Bad:
<SheetContent>
  <!-- Always from right -->
</SheetContent>

Description: shadcn forms integrate with react-hook-form
Do: Use Form components with useForm hook
Code Good:
const form = useForm<FormValues>({
  resolver: zodResolver(formSchema)
})
<Form {...form}>
  <FormField
    control={form.control}
    name="email"
    render={({ field }) => (
      <FormItem>
        <FormLabel>Email</FormLabel>
        <FormControl>
          <Input {...field} />
        </FormControl>
        <FormMessage />
      </FormItem>
    )}
  />
</Form>
Code Bad: setEmail(e.target.value)} /> {errors.email && {errors.email}}

Description: Use Zod for type-safe form validation
Do: Define Zod schema and use zodResolver

Description: Show validation errors below form fields
Do: Use FormMessage component
Code Good:
<FormField
  name="email"
  render={({ field }) => (
    <FormItem>
      <FormLabel>Email</FormLabel>
      <FormControl>
        <Input {...field} />
      </FormControl>
      <FormDescription>Your email address</FormDescription>
      <FormMessage />
    </FormItem>
  )}
/>
Code Bad:
{form.formState.errors.email && (

{form.formState.errors.email.message}

)}

Description: Select provides dropdown for single selection
Do: Use Select with proper value handling
Code Good:
<Select value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <SelectTrigger>
    <SelectValue placeholder="Select option" />
  </SelectTrigger>
  <SelectContent>
    <SelectItem value="a">Option A</SelectItem>
    <SelectItem value="b">Option B</SelectItem>
  </SelectContent>
</Select>
Code Bad:
<select
  value={value}
  onChange={(e) => setValue(e.target.value)}
>
  <option value="a">Option A</option>
</select>
// Unstyled, inconsistent

Description: Use SelectGroup and SelectLabel for organization
Do: Group options with labels
Code Good:
<SelectContent>
  <SelectGroup>
    <SelectLabel>Fruits</SelectLabel>
    <SelectItem value="apple">Apple</SelectItem>
    <SelectItem value="banana">Banana</SelectItem>
  </SelectGroup>
  <SelectGroup>
    <SelectLabel>Vegetables</SelectLabel>
    <SelectItem value="carrot">Carrot</SelectItem>
  </SelectGroup>
</SelectContent>
Code Bad:
<SelectContent>
  <SelectItem value="apple">Apple</SelectItem>
  <SelectItem value="banana">Banana</SelectItem>
  <SelectItem value="carrot">Carrot</SelectItem>
</SelectContent>
// Hard to scan

Description: Command provides searchable command palette interface
Do: Use Command for search and filter interfaces
Code Good:
<Command>
  <CommandInput placeholder="Search..." />
  <CommandList>
    <CommandEmpty>No results found.</CommandEmpty>
    <CommandGroup heading="Suggestions">
      <CommandItem>Calendar</CommandItem>
      <CommandItem>Search</CommandItem>
    </CommandGroup>
  </CommandList>
</Command>
Code Bad:
filterItems(e.target.value)} /> {filteredItems.map(item => (
select(item)}>{item}
))}

Description: Combine Command with Popover for combobox pattern
Do: Wrap Command in Popover for dropdown search
Code Good:
<Popover open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <PopoverTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">
      {value || 'Select...'}
    </Button>
  </PopoverTrigger>
  <PopoverContent>
    <Command>
      <CommandInput />
      <CommandList>
        <CommandItem onSelect={() => setValue('...')}>
          ...
        </CommandItem>
      </CommandList>
    </Command>
  </PopoverContent>
</Popover>
Code Bad:
<Command className="mb-4">
  <!-- Always visible, takes space -->
</Command>

Guideline: Use Table for simple data display
Description: Table component provides styled table markup
Do: Use Table components for semantic tables
Code Good: Name Email {users.map(user => ( {user.name} {user.email} ))}
Code Bad:
Name
Email
{users.map(user => ( <>
{user.name}
{user.email}
))}

Guideline: Use TanStack Table for complex tables
Description: DataTable combines Table with TanStack Table for features
Do: Use @tanstack/react-table for sorting filtering pagination
Code Good:
import { useReactTable, getCoreRowModel } from '@tanstack/react-table'
const table = useReactTable({
  data,
  columns,
  getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(),
  getSortedRowModel: getSortedRowModel()
})

Description: Column definitions should be typed and memoized
Do: Use ColumnDef type and useMemo

Description: Add pagination controls for large datasets
Do: Use pagination state and controls
Code Bad:
{allData.map(item => ( ... ))}
// Renders thousands of rows

Description: Tabs organize related content into switchable panels
Do: Use Tabs for mutually exclusive content views
Code Good:
<Tabs defaultValue="account">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="account">Account</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="password">Password</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="account">
    <AccountSettings />
  </TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="password">
    <PasswordSettings />
  </TabsContent>
</Tabs>
Code Bad: {tab === 'account' && } {tab === 'password' && }

Description: Use value and onValueChange for controlled tabs
Do: Control tab state when needed programmatically
Code Bad:
<Tabs defaultValue="account">
  {/* Can't change tab programmatically */}
</Tabs>

Description: Accordion shows collapsible content sections
Do: Use Accordion for FAQ or grouped content
Code Good:
<Accordion type="single" collapsible>
  <AccordionItem value="item-1">
    <AccordionTrigger>Question 1?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      Answer to question 1.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem value="item-2">
    <AccordionTrigger>Question 2?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      Answer to question 2.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

Description: Choose single or multiple expansion behavior
Do: Set type based on UX requirements
Code Good:
<Accordion type="multiple">
  {/* Multiple items can be open */}
</Accordion>
<Accordion type="single" collapsible>
  {/* One item, can close all */}
</Accordion>
Code Bad:
<Accordion type="single">
  {/* Must always have one open */}
</Accordion>

Do: Use toast from sonner package

Description: Configure Popover alignment and side
Do: Set side and align props for proper placement
Code Good:
<PopoverContent side="right" align="start">
  {/* Opens to right, aligned to top */}
</PopoverContent>
<PopoverContent side="bottom" align="center">
  {/* Opens below, centered */}
</PopoverContent>
Code Bad:
<PopoverContent>
  {/* Default position may overflow */}
</PopoverContent>

Description: DropdownMenu provides accessible dropdown menus
Do: Use DropdownMenu for action lists
Code Good:
<DropdownMenu>
  <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="ghost" size="icon">
      <MoreHorizontal />
    </Button>
  </DropdownMenuTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuContent>
    <DropdownMenuItem>Edit</DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuItem>Duplicate</DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuSeparator />
    <DropdownMenuItem className="text-red-600">
      Delete
    </DropdownMenuItem>
  </DropdownMenuContent>
</DropdownMenu>
Code Bad:
<Popover>
  <PopoverTrigger>...</PopoverTrigger>
  <PopoverContent>
    <button>Edit</button>
    <button>Delete</button>
  </PopoverContent>
</Popover>
// No menu semantics

Guideline: Add keyboard shortcuts to menu items
Description: Display keyboard shortcuts in dropdown items
Do: Use DropdownMenuShortcut component
Code Good:
<DropdownMenuItem>
  New Tab
  <DropdownMenuShortcut>Cmd+T</DropdownMenuShortcut>
</DropdownMenuItem>
<DropdownMenuItem>
  New Window
  <DropdownMenuShortcut>Cmd+N</DropdownMenuShortcut>
</DropdownMenuItem>
Code Bad:
<DropdownMenuItem>New Tab (Cmd+T)</DropdownMenuItem>
// Inconsistent formatting

Description: DropdownMenuSub creates hierarchical menus
Do: Use DropdownMenuSub for nested options
Don't: Flatten all options or use modal
Code Good:
<DropdownMenuSub>
  <DropdownMenuSubTrigger>More Tools</DropdownMenuSubTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuSubContent>
    <DropdownMenuItem>Save Page As...</DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuItem>Create Shortcut...</DropdownMenuItem>
  </DropdownMenuSubContent>
</DropdownMenuSub>
Code Bad:
<DropdownMenuItem
  onClick={() => setShowMoreTools(true)}
>
  More Tools...
</DropdownMenuItem>
// Opens separate modal

Description: Tooltip shows brief text on hover
Do: Use Tooltip for icon buttons and truncated text
Code Good: Settings
Code Bad:
<Tooltip>
  <TooltipTrigger>...</TooltipTrigger>
  <TooltipContent>
    <form>
      <Input />
      <Button>Submit</Button>
    </form>
  </TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>
// Use Popover instead

Description: TooltipProvider manages tooltip behavior globally
Do: Add single TooltipProvider near app root
Code Bad:
// Each tooltip
<TooltipProvider>
  <Tooltip>...</Tooltip>
</TooltipProvider>
<TooltipProvider>
  <Tooltip>...</Tooltip>
</TooltipProvider>

Description: Skeleton provides content placeholder during loading
Do: Use Skeleton matching content shape
Don't: Use spinner for all loading states
Code Good:
<Card>
  <CardHeader>
    {loading ? (
      <>
        <Skeleton className="h-4 w-[250px]" />
        <Skeleton className="h-4 w-[200px]" />
      </>
    ) : (
      <>
        <CardTitle>{title}</CardTitle>
        <CardDescription>{desc}</CardDescription>
      </>
    )}
  </CardHeader>
</Card>
Code Bad:
<Card>
  {loading ? (
    <Spinner />
  ) : (
    <CardHeader>...</CardHeader>
  )}
</Card>
// Layout shift when loaded

Description: Skeleton dimensions should match loaded content
Do: Size skeletons to match actual content
Code Bad:
<Skeleton className="h-20 w-full" />
// Doesn't match card layout

Description: AlertDialog interrupts user for important confirmations
Do: Use AlertDialog for destructive action confirmations
Code Good:
<AlertDialog>
  <AlertDialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button variant="destructive">Delete</Button>
  </AlertDialogTrigger>
  <AlertDialogContent>
    <AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogTitle>Delete item?</AlertDialogTitle>
      <AlertDialogDescription>
        This action cannot be undone.
      </AlertDialogDescription>
    </AlertDialogHeader>
    <AlertDialogFooter>
      <AlertDialogCancel>Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>
      <AlertDialogAction onClick={handleDelete}>
        Delete
      </AlertDialogAction>
    </AlertDialogFooter>
  </AlertDialogContent>
</AlertDialog>
Code Bad:
Are you sure?

 // Can dismiss without action

Description: AlertDialog should not close without explicit choice
Do: Use AlertDialogCancel and AlertDialogAction
Code Good:
<AlertDialogContent>
  <AlertDialogFooter>
    <AlertDialogCancel>Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>
    <AlertDialogAction>Continue</AlertDialogAction>
  </AlertDialogFooter>
</AlertDialogContent>
// Must click button to close
Code Bad:
<Dialog>
  <DialogContent onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}>
    {/* Can still click backdrop */}
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

Do: Use Sidebar components for main nav

...
{children}

Do: Use useSidebar hook for collapse control

Description: shadcn charts are built on Recharts
Do: Use Chart components with Recharts
Don't: Use other chart libraries or canvas
Code Good:
import { ChartContainer, ChartTooltip } from '@/components/ui/chart'
<ChartContainer config={chartConfig}>
  <BarChart data={data}>
    <Bar dataKey="value" fill="var(--color-primary)" />
    <ChartTooltip />
  </BarChart>
</ChartContainer>

Description: Use CSS variables for chart colors
Do: Define colors in chartConfig with CSS variables
Code Bad:

  dataKey="revenue"
  fill="#4f46e5"
/>

Open

  </Button>
</DropdownMenuTrigger>

/>
